program_en: B.Sc. in Earth and Environmental Sciences
program_chi: 地球與環境科學理學士
learning_outcomes:
1. Major
Programme: K
– A solid theoretical foundation in the Earth and Environmental Sciences not
only as defined disciplines but also their inter-dependent as well as
synergistic nature for developing a broad perspective of the interaction between
humankind and the natural world. S
– Competency in generic (literacy, numeracy, communication) and
problem-solving (team work, critical thinking, technical writing) as well as
disciplinary skills, with particular emphasis on quantitative and analytical
approaches in the Earth and Environmental Sciences that are relevant to the
contemporary job market. V
– An inquisitive mind equipped with the necessary knowledge, skills and
perspective for tackling the environmental challenges to long-term
sustainability of human society at both the local and global levels. K
= Knowledge outcomes S
= Skills outcomes V
= Values and attitude outcomes
study_scheme:
Earth and Environmental Sciences Earth
and Environmental Sciences Applicable to
students admitted in 2025-26
There are three streams of
specialization: 1) Atmospheric Science, 2) Geology and Geophysics, and 3)
Environmental Science and Technology. The stream selected by the student
will be printed on the transcript. Students admitted to the Earth and
Environmental Sciences Major Programme through the broad-based admission
scheme may apply at their final year of attendance to specialize in any
of the streams and select the relevant courses as prescribed below.
Major Programme
Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:

In addition to fulfilling
the above Major Programme Requirement, students meeting the criteria as
specified by the Faculty can take the following stream offered by the
Faculty:
Science, Technology and
Research Stream
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 12 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  One Faculty Package Course:  |  3
Choose from the remaining
group of the Faculty Package that has not been used to fulfill the
Faculty Package Requirement
(b)  |  Research Courses:  |  6
STAR2000, 3000, 4000[c]
(c)  |  Seminar Courses:  |  3
STAR2050, 3050, 4050
2.  |  Experiential Learning:  |  ---
At least 4 consecutive
weeks of outside Hong Kong exposure[d]
Total:  |  12
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  BCHE, BIOL, CHEM, CSCI, EEEN, EESC, ELEG, ENGG, ENSC,
ESSC, GPAD, GRMD, IERG, LAWS, LSCI, MAEG, MATH, MBTE, PHPC, PHYS, RMSC,
STAT, URSP courses at 2000 and above level will be included in the calculation
of Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Students are strongly encouraged to take one more
course in the remaining group to fulfill part of the Electives
requirement in 3(d) in order to broaden their exposure to all areas of
science.
[b]  |  Students in the Atmospheric Science Stream and Geology
and Geophysics Stream are strongly encouraged to take courses under CHEM,
CSCI, EEEN, ELEG, ENGG, IERG, MAEG, MATH, PHYS, RMSC and STAT to
strengthen their quantitative background and partly fulfill this
requirement.
[c]  |  Students may select research-oriented course(s), as
approved by the Major Programme, to substitute up to 4 units for
fulfillment of Research Courses requirement.
[d]  |  Students must complete any
exchange/research/internship programme(s) offered by the University,
Colleges, the Faculty of Science or Major Programme, as approved by the
Major Programme, to fulfill the Experiential Learning requirement.
Students are responsible for the extra costs incurred in the
exchange/research/internship programme(s).

Minor
Programme Title Earth
and Environmental Sciences
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 20 units of courses, with at least 6 units at 3000
or about level, as follows[a]:
Units
1. Required Courses:
EESC2010, 2020, 2270  |  9
2. Elective Courses:
Any 11 units of courses from the following, with
at least 3 units at 3000 level and 3 units at 4000 level: BIOL2210, 3710, 4012, 4230, 4260, 4420,
EESC2160, 2515, 2800, 3100, 3120, 3130, 3140, 3200, 3220, 3300, 3320,
3415, 3510, 3520, 3540, 3600, 3601, 3800, 4010, 4020, 4030, 4040, 4110,
4120, 4130, 4140, 4150, 4160, 4170, 4180, 4210, 4220, 4230, 4240, 4250,
4260, 4270, 4280, 4325, 4330, 4335, 4350, 4510, 4520, 4540, 4550  |  11
Total:  |  20
Explanatory
Note:
[a]  |  No more than 9 units of
courses taken to fulfill the requirements of the students’ Major
Programme(s) and other Minor Programme(s) respectively can be used to
fulfill the requirement of this Minor Programme.
